1.1: PAUL, and Sil-va’nus, and Ti-mo’theus, unto the church of the Thes-salo’ni- ans which is in God the Father and in our LORD Jesus Christ: Grace be unto you and peace, from God our Father, and the LORD Jesus Christ.
1.2: We give thanks to God always for you all, remembering you continually in our prayers;
1.3: Mentioning before God the Father the works of your faith and the labor of your love, and the patience of your hope in our LORD Jesus Christ.
1.4: For we know that you are the elected ones, my brethren, and beloved of God.
1.5: For our preaching to you was not in words only, but also in power, and with the Holy Spirit, and with sincere assurance; and you know also how we lived among you for your sakes.
1.6: And you became followers of us, and of our LORD, for you welcomed the word with much tribulation and with joy of the Holy Spirit:
1.7: Thus you have become examples to all the believers in Mac-e-do’ni-a and A-cha’ia.
1.8: Not only have you sounded out the word of our LORD in Mac-e-do’ni-a and A-cha’ia but also, in every place your faith in God has been heard so that we need not speak anything about you.
1.9: For these people themselves relate how we entered first among you, and how you turned to God from idols, to serve the living and true God;
1.10: To wait for his Son from heaven, even Jesus, whom he raised from the dead, for it is he who will deliver us from the wrath to come.

2.1: SO you yourselves, my brethren, know that our entrance among you was not in vain:
2.2: But from the beginning we suffered and as you know were treated shamefully, at Philip’pi; then with more struggle but with confidence in our God, we preached to you the gospel of Christ.
2.3: For our comfort did not spring from deception, nor from uncleanness, nor from enticing speech:
2.4: But just as we have been examined by God to be entrusted with his gospel, even so we speak, not to please men, but to please God who searches our hearts.
2.5: And we have never used flattering words, as you know, to conceal greed; God is witness:
2.6: And we have not sought support from men, neither from you, nor from others, when we could have been burdensome on you for our maintenance, as the apostles of Christ should be.
2.7: But we were meek when we were among you, and like a foster mother who loves her children:
2.8: Likewise, we are affectionately desirous to give you, not only the gospel of God, but even our lives, because you were dear to us.
2.9: For you remember, brethren, that we labored hard, working night and day, with our hands, so that we would not burden you.
2.10: You are witnesses, and God also, how we preached to you the gospel of God, purely and righteously, and we lived blamelessly among all the believers.
2.11: You know how we exhorted and encouraged and charged every one of you, as a father does his children,
2.12: And we bore the testimony to you, so that you may live a life worthy of God, who has called you to his kingdom and glory.
2.13: For this cause also we thank God continually, because, when you received the word of God which you heard from us you received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which works effectively in you who believe.
2.14: For you, my brethren, have taken the pattern of the churches of God in Judæa which are in Christ Jesus: for you also have suffered from the people of your own tribe, even as they have suffered from the Jews;
2.15: Who both killed the LORD Jesus Christ, and their own prophets, and have persecuted us; and they do not please God, and are against all men;
2.16: Forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they might be saved, adding this to their sins always; but the wrath of God is upon them to the uttermost.
2.17: But we, brethren, have been deprived of your affection for a little while, yet only in presence and not in heart, so we have with great love vigorously endeavored to see your faces.
2.18: And we have wanted to come to you; I, Paul, tried several times but Satan hindered me.
2.19: For what is our hope, our joy, or crown of our glorying? Is it not you in the presence of our LORD Jesus when he comes?
2.20: For you are our glory and our joy.

3.1: AND because we could no longer with stand these obstacles, we decided to remain at Athens alone;
3.2: And send Ti-mo’the-us, our brother, a minister of God, and our helper in the gospel of Christ, to sustain you and comfort you concerning your faith:
3.3: So that no man among you might be disheartened by these tribulations: for you, yourselves, know that this is our destiny.
3.4: For verily when we were with you, we told you before, that we should suffer tribulations; even as it has come to pass, and as you know.
3.5: For this reason also, when I could no longer wait, I sent to know your faith, lest by some means the tempter had tempted you, and our labor was in vain.
3.6: But now since Ti-mo’the-us has returned to us from you, and brought us good tidings of your faith and love, and that you have good remembrance of us always, longing to see us, just as we also long to see you:
3.7: Therefore, our brethren, we have been comforted by you, in the midst of all our distress and tribulations because of your faith:
3.8: Now we can live happily, if you stand firm in our LORD.
3.9: What thanks can we offer to God for you, for all the joy with which we rejoice for your sakes,
3.10: Except, before God to abundantly offer supplication, night and day, to see your faces, and to complete that which is lacking in your faith?
3.11: Now may God, our Father, and our LORD Jesus Christ, direct our journey unto you,
3.12: And may the LORD increase and enrich your love toward one another and toward all men, even as we love you:
3.13: And may he strengthen your hearts to be without blemish in holiness before God our Father, at the coming of our LORD Jesus Christ with all his saints.

4.1: FROM this time then, my brethren, we beseech you and entreat you earnestly by our LORD Jesus, that as you have been taught by us how you ought to live and to please God, so you will increase more and more.
4.2: For you know what commandments we gave you by our LORD Jesus.
4.3: For this is the will of God, even your sanctification, that you should abstain from fornication:
4.4: That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour;
4.5: And not through the passion of lust, even as the rest of the Gentiles who know not God:
4.6: And that no man overreach to transgress and defraud his brother in this matter; because our LORD is the avenger of all such, as we have also forewarned you and testified.
4.7: For God has not called you unto uncleanness but unto holiness.
4.8: Therefore, he who does an injustice, does not wrong man but God who has also given unto you his Holy Spirit.
4.9: Now concerning brotherly love, you do not need me to write to you for you yourselves are taught by God to love one another.
4.10: And indeed you show it toward all the brethren who are in Mac-e-do’ni-a; but I beseech you, my brethren, that you increase your love more and more;
4.11: And that you endeavor to be quiet, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you;
4.12: That you may lead a life of good example toward outsiders, so that you depend on no man.
4.13: Now I want you to know, my brethren, that you should not grieve over those who are dead, as those do who have no hope.
4.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so those who have died in Jesus, God will bring with him.
4.15: For this we say to you by the very word of our LORD, that we who are alive and remain unto the coming of our LORD shall not overtake those who are dead.
4.16: For our LORD himself shall descend from heaven with a shout and the voice of the archangel, and with the trumpet of God; and those who died in Christ will rise first.
4.17: Then we, who are alive, and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet our LORD in the air: and so shall we ever be with our LORD.
4.18: Wherefore comfort one another with these words.

5.1: BUT of the times and seasons, my brethren, you have no need that I should write to you.
5.2: For you yourselves know perfectly well that the day of our LORD comes just like a thief in the night.
5.3: For when they shall say: Peace and tranquility; then sudden destruction will come upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape.
5.4: But you, my brethren, are not in darkness that that day shall overtake you as a thief.
5.5: You are all the children of light, and the children of the day; and you are not the children of the night, nor the children of darkness.
5.6: Therefore let us not sleep, as do others, but let us watch and be sober.
5.7: For those who sleep, sleep in the night: and those who are drunken are drunken in the night.
5.8: But let us who are the children of the day, be alert, putting on the breastplate of faith and love and for a helmet put on the hope of salvation.
5.9: For God has not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation through our LORD Jesus Christ,
5.10: Who died for us, that whether we awake or sleep, we shall live together with him.
5.11: Wherefore comfort one another and edify one another, even as also you do.
5.12: We beseech you, my brethren, to respect those who labor among you, and admonish you in our LORD and teach you;
5.13: That you esteem them very highly in love, and be at peace with them for their work’s sake.
5.14: Now we beseech you, my brethren, correct those who offend, comfort those who lack courage, bear the burdens of the weak, and be patient toward all men.
5.15: See that none of you render evil for evil; but always follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all men.
5.18: In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Jesus Christ concerning you.
5.21: Prove all things, uphold that which is good.
5.22: Abstain from every sort of evil.
5.23: May the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and may your spirit and soul and body be preserved without blemish to the coming of our LORD Jesus Christ.
5.24: Faithful is he who has called you and he will keep his word.
5.26: Salute all our brethren with a holy kiss.
5.27: I adjure you by our LORD that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.
5.28: The grace of our LORD Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
